hero.manifesto: Software developer with five years of experience in application architecture for both public and private sectors, as well as technical leadership. Throughout my career, I progressed from software developer to Technical Lead, contributing to architectural design, solution implementation, and the technical leadership of projects for clients across a wide range of industries and levels of complexity. Today, I focus on developing and evolving modern applications, prioritizing solutions that best meet client needs, software quality, engineering best practices, and a value-driven approach to software delivery.

stats.positions: Positions — Junior I to Tech Lead

timeline.title: Advanced SQL
timeline.desc: Advanced data structures, query syntax, and database optimization.

timeline.title: Java Spring Boot / Quarkus
timeline.desc: From fundamentals to advanced Java with Spring Boot and Quarkus for production applications.

timeline.title: Tech Lead
timeline.desc: I provide technical leadership for the team, serving as the architectural reference point and decision-maker on stack, design patterns, and the structure of new systems. I led two simultaneous rebuilds of state government systems, modernizing Groovy/Grails applications into React, TypeScript, and Java Spring Boot, and I own the definition of the REST contracts between frontend and backend. Day to day, my responsibilities mean I write somewhat less code than before, since I increasingly coordinate the process itself — writing and refining tasks, running daily standups, sprint planning, refinements, and retrospectives, aligning estimates and scope with the team, talking directly with clients about deadlines, deliveries, and staging approvals, reviewing code, and keeping the CI/CD pipelines and the development, staging, and production environments always running. I also onboard new developers to the team and take part in the company's strategic decisions.

timeline.title: Angular
timeline.desc: Introduction and advanced use of Angular, PrimeNG, and PrimeFaces in production applications.

timeline.title: Ignite React
timeline.desc: Advanced React with Vanilla JS and a focus on optimizing production applications.

timeline.title: Mid-Level Software Developer
timeline.desc: This is where I went from executing to deciding. I architected the company's first rebuild projects, defining the stack and repository structure for teams that would grow in the years ahead. I ran technical hiring interviews, reviewed other developers' code, and started writing and refining tasks for the team, while also expanding into the Angular framework on corporate projects. The work started splitting between code and process — and process increasingly became part of my day to day. Strategic decisions from the company combined with my own professional alignment pushed me to keep growing in seniority. In parallel, I worked on a few smaller internal projects where we automated processes that would later benefit my own management work.

timeline.title: Junior Software Developer III
timeline.desc: I started stepping away from the keyboard for a few moments: I joined my first architectural decision meetings, still as a contributor rather than a decision-maker. In parallel, I deepened my knowledge of the stack we were using for projects at the time (React/Angular on the frontend; Java Spring Boot/Quarkus on the backend; PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, and Redis for databases). The goal was to execute tasks faster without compromising the final result. I began following the full cycle of agile ceremonies — daily standups, sprint planning, refinements, reviews, and retrospectives — as an active participant, not just a task executor.

timeline.title: Junior Software Developer II
timeline.desc: At this stage, I started earning the trust of more experienced developers and was assigned to a CRM project a bit more complex than usual — a system that already existed and was already a proven success story before I arrived. I became a key part of implementing entire features, integrating authentication via LDAP/Active Directory, writing stored procedures in SQL Server, and building reusable React components in TypeScript, always in direct contact with the Node.js and Java Spring Boot backend. I worked side by side with the QA team fixing bugs, bugfixes, and hotfixes as tickets came straight from production — spotting N+1 queries that were freezing entire screens and writing unit tests to prevent regressions. I also took my first steps into mobile development with React Native. I was still early in my career, but I was already showing the team and management my potential within the projects and my drive to solve problems.

timeline.title: Junior Software Developer I
timeline.desc: I started at the company from the basics, working on just one or two projects at a time and focused on simpler tasks: fixing visual bugs, adjusting simple endpoints, straightforward CRUDs, and one-off screens. I attended daily standups mostly as a listener, reporting my status to more experienced developers. At times during the day, I paired with colleagues who had been there longer — that's how I learned the system's business rules and got a hands-on understanding of a developer's day-to-day routine.

timeline.title: GoStack React
timeline.desc: Introduction and deep dive into React for production applications, consolidating fundamental and advanced concepts.

timeline.title: Technical Degree in Information Technology
timeline.desc: Technical program covering programming fundamentals, infrastructure, networking, databases, and front-end/back-end development (RESTful APIs).
